laws.title: Public Law 38-264 | Organized Crime and Racketeering Modernization Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act repeals CRACK Act titles V and VI and creates an ongoing racketeering framework, but the record still resolves it as note-level history rather than a clean positive-law rewrite.

current_implementation.detail: The branch keeps the act at the Title 18 anchor, but the entry still carries dump and link material instead of a concise history note.

laws.recommended_action: Keep the repeal history and racketeering framework as a concise statutory note, and remove the dump/link material.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act repeals CRACK Act titles V and VI and creates an ongoing racketeering framework, but the record still resolves it as note-level history rather than a clean positive-law rewrite.

laws.title: Public Law 38-265 | Employment Clarity and Federal Workforce Modernization Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act repeals several prior employment laws and lays out a continuing workforce framework, but no precise positive-law target is identified in the record.

current_implementation.detail: The note is in Title 5 territory, but it still carries dump and link material and should stay concise.

laws.recommended_action: Retain a concise statutory note at the Title 5 employment anchor and preserve the repeal history only.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act repeals several prior employment laws and lays out a continuing workforce framework, but no precise positive-law target is identified in the record.

laws.title: Public Law 38-266 | Speedy Trial Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: Sections 6(a) and 6(b) directly amend Public Law 4-32, while the remaining provisions can stay as history material.
targets: Pub. L. 22-162 / 18 U.S. Code Chapter 208 Part II

current_implementation.detail: The current note block is still parked at the Title 18 anchor, but the source includes a determinable amendment to the prior Public Law 4-32 text.

laws.recommended_action: Apply the SEC. 6 amendments to the prior-law chain and keep only a concise history note.
laws.chronology_conclusion: Sections 6(a) and 6(b) directly amend Public Law 4-32, while the remaining provisions can stay as history material.

laws.title: Public Law 39-267 | Reform Sentencing Act
laws.status_basis: The source repeals PL 34-251 and adds ongoing sentencing rules and a multi-charge cap, but the record does not resolve a safe positive-law section to rewrite.

current_implementation.detail: The branch still uses a note at the Title 18 anchor, but it keeps the repeal history and sentencing framework as a note-only treatment.

laws.recommended_action: Keep the repeal history for PL 34-251 as a concise note; if codified later, assign a precise Title 18 placement.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source repeals PL 34-251 and adds ongoing sentencing rules and a multi-charge cap, but the record does not resolve a safe positive-law section to rewrite.

laws.title: Public Law 40-268 | Big Beautiful Budget Act
laws.status_basis: This is a time-bound appropriations act; its allocations and oversight instructions are not permanent Code text.

current_implementation.detail: The act is sitting in a Title 31 anchor as a full dump, but it should not be carried as permanent Code text.

laws.recommended_action: Exclude the appropriations and oversight provisions from permanent Code text and keep only a brief history note if needed.

laws.title: Public Law 41-271 | The Great Change Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act combines repeals, direct amendments, transfers, and conforming amendments across several existing laws.
targets: WDCBA; National Commission on Judicial Activity, Efficiency and Accountability Act; OOP

current_implementation.detail: The current note block remains at the Title 18 anchor, but the source demands multiple direct amendments rather than a note-only treatment.

laws.recommended_action: Convert the direct amendments in Titles II-VI into the affected Code or prior-law text and keep only a concise residual note.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act combines repeals, direct amendments, transfers, and conforming amendments across several existing laws.
